name: Gaxos.ai Inc.

profile.description: Gaxos.ai Inc. is a micro-cap company developing AI-powered applications across multiple segments: Gaxos Labs (UnGPT.ai text humanizer, Art-Gen.AI image/video creation, Bible Pray AI spiritual app, AI Sales Coaching Platform), Gaxos Health (RNK Health subsidiary providing medications and wellness products), and a 19.99% stake in America First Defense.AI (counter-drone systems). Formerly The NFT Gaming Company, the firm rebranded in January 2024. Headquartered in Roseland, New Jersey with 3 full-time employees and Q1 2026 revenue of $1.81M against a net loss of $2.47M.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (0/3): Gaxos.ai has pivoted through multiple business models since incorporation in 2021 — from NFT gaming to AI gaming to health services to defense investments — without establishing sustained success in any domain [1]. Revenue grew from $4,027 (FY2024) to $1.93M (FY2025), but the majority ($1.25M of $1.81M Q1 2026) comes from the RNK Health administrative services subsidiary, not AI products. The rapid pivoting suggests trend-chasing rather than genuine innovation. AI Deployment (1/2): Gaxos has shipped multiple AI-powered products: UnGPT.ai (text humanizer, launched May 2025), Art-Gen.AI (image/video creation, launched August 2025), Bible Pray AI (spiritual app, launched December 2025), and an AI Sales Coaching Platform funded by Amazon AWS with Caylent as development partner [1]. New features added April 2026 include AI Music Generation, AI Chat, and AI 3D Model Creation. These are shipping products, though AI subscription revenue was only $563K in Q1 2026. Market Share (1/2): ByteDance/BytePlus partnership (February 2026) for preferred pricing and early access to video-generation models, and America First Defense.AI's acceptance into Anduril's Lattice Program (March 2026) and Naval Postgraduate School drone hijacker license show some traction in emerging niches [1]. However, market position is negligible against major competitors. Product Pipeline (1/2): AI Sales Coaching Platform with additional AWS funding (June 2026), Defense.AI with Anduril Lattice integration, and Art-Gen monetization via ByteDance partnership provide multiple potential growth vectors, though none has achieved meaningful revenue scale [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D spending of $446K in Q1 2026 is minimal, while SG&A of $3.91M (of which $2.77M is advertising/marketing) dwarfs R&D by nearly 9:1, indicating a marketing-driven rather than innovation-driven business [1].

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): Gaxos.ai's tagline 'Empowering possibilities with AI' and 'Transforming Industries with AI' is generic and interchangeable with any AI company [2]. The company's rapid pivoting through NFTs, AI gaming, health, defense, and spiritual apps undermines any coherent purpose narrative. Community Impact (0/2): No evidence of charitable programs or community investment was identified. Employee Retention (0/2): With only 3 full-time employees and a CEO receiving $800K in compensation (Q1 2026 alone: $400K salary + $400K discretionary bonus) relative to a $12.5M market cap company, there is insufficient organizational substance to evaluate team dynamics [2]. The CEO-to-employee compensation ratio raises governance concerns. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO Vadim Mats maintains significant equity holdings, and no insider selling was identified. However, the heavy dilution via ATM offerings (shares grew from 7.12M to 10.22M in Q1 2026 alone, with 4.5M warrants outstanding) effectively reduces insider ownership stakes without direct sales [1].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on limited available metrics. Only three metrics are calculable: ROE of -35.8% scores 0.0 (below the 0% floor), profit margin of -131.3% scores 0.0 (below the 0% floor), and revenue growth YoY is extremely high (from near-zero base) scoring 1.0. Weighted average of 1.0/4 weight units = 0.25, yielding Infrastructure 2/10 [4]. D/E is not applicable (no debt), Forward P/E is not calculable (negative earnings), and PEG is unavailable. Liquidity check: average daily volume of 2,511,806 at $1.22 = $3.06M, passing the $1M floor. Q1 2026 operating expenses of $4.36M against revenue of $1.81M produce a net loss of $2.47M [1]. Cash of $1.51M plus $10.2M in short-term corporate bond investments provide approximately 12 months of runway at the current burn rate, but continued ATM dilution is required for survival.

ai_resilience.label: HIGH RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (2/9): Paradoxically, Gaxos.ai is an AI company that faces high AI disruption risk because its products (UnGPT.ai, Art-Gen.AI, Bible Pray AI, AI Sales Coaching) are thin application-layer wrappers around commoditizing generative AI capabilities that major platforms like OpenAI, Google, ByteDance, and Unity are integrating natively [1]. The company has no proprietary AI models, no unique training data, and no scale advantages with 3 employees. Art-Gen.AI relies on ByteDance/BytePlus video-generation models via a partnership that gives ByteDance control over the underlying technology. As foundation model providers expand into vertical applications, the middleman position becomes increasingly untenable. The one defensive asset is the America First Defense.AI investment (Anduril Lattice Program, drone hijacker technology), which involves physical defense hardware less susceptible to AI commoditization, but this is only a 19.99% minority stake [1]. AI Opportunity (1/1): Gaxos generates AI-related revenue — Q1 2026 AI subscription revenue of $563K from UnGPT.ai, Art-Gen.AI, and other tools, and additional revenue from the AI Sales Coaching Platform funded by AWS [1].

infinite_game.summary: Gaxos.ai exhibits strongly finite-game characteristics: rapid pivots between trending sectors (NFTs to AI to health to defense to spiritual apps), reliance on dilutive ATM financing to survive, CEO compensation of $800K in Q1 alone against a $12.5M market cap, and a scattered strategy across six different product areas with 3 employees. The company's behavior pattern suggests playing to survive each quarter by chasing retail investor interest rather than building enduring value.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (0/3): The company has changed its name, business model, and target market multiple times since incorporation in 2021 — from NFT gaming to AI gaming to health services to defense to spiritual apps to sales coaching [1]. 'Empowering possibilities with AI' is a generic tagline, not a Just Cause. No coherent purpose emerges from the pattern of pivoting toward whatever sector generates retail investor momentum. Sinek would characterize this as a company that does not know why it exists beyond generating stock price catalysts.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (0/2): With only 3 full-time employees, the company lacks the organizational scale for meaningful team dynamics [2]. The CEO's $800K compensation in Q1 2026 ($400K salary + $400K discretionary bonus) against a $12.5M market cap and 3-person headcount raises questions about governance and resource allocation. The 43% share count increase in Q1 2026 alone (7.12M to 10.22M via ATM offering) dilutes all stakeholders. No evidence of organizational culture, team empowerment, or employee-centered policies exists at this scale.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): Gaxos.ai competes against massively better-resourced rivals in every segment — OpenAI and Google in AI content generation, established health platforms in wellness, and defense primes (plus Anduril itself) in defense technology [1]. Rather than learning from these rivals' strengths to improve, the company appears to pivot between sectors seeking gaps where large competitors have not yet focused, a finite-game approach to competition.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): The company demonstrates extreme adaptability — pivoting from NFT gaming to AI gaming to health services to defense investments to spiritual apps to sales coaching within 36 months [1]. The ByteDance partnership, AWS-funded sales coaching platform, and America First Defense.AI's Anduril Lattice acceptance show some ability to identify emerging opportunities. However, this flexibility reflects lack of strategic commitment rather than purposeful reinvention. None of the pivots was driven by a Just Cause — they were driven by market trends.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (0/2): Rather than demonstrating courage to commit to any single domain, Gaxos.ai appears to follow market trends — pivoting to AI when it became popular, adding health services, acquiring a defense stake, launching a spiritual app, then a sales coaching tool [1]. The $2.9M investment in America First Defense.AI consumed a significant portion of available capital for a speculative minority stake. The CEO's $800K compensation in Q1 against massive losses suggests leadership priorities that favor personal enrichment over long-term company building [2].

investment_thesis: Gaxos.ai offers speculative upside through its diversified AI portfolio with multiple catalysts: ByteDance/BytePlus partnership for Art-Gen monetization (+35% stock surge on announcement), Amazon AWS-funded AI Sales Coaching Platform (+42% surge), and America First Defense.AI's acceptance into Anduril's Lattice Program with Naval Postgraduate School drone hijacker license (+46% surge) [1]. Q1 2026 revenue of $1.81M (vs $23.7K in Q1 2025) demonstrates real revenue traction. At a $12.5M market cap with $11.7M in cash and short-term investments, the company trades near its cash value, providing downside protection. The RNK Health subsidiary generated $1.25M in Q1 2026 revenue and could scale further. Any single product area achieving meaningful scale — particularly defense via Anduril — could generate outsized returns from the current micro-cap base.
thesis_continued: The bear case is overwhelming: Gaxos.ai has 3 employees, a $12.5M market cap, -131% profit margins, and has pivoted its business model five times since 2021 [1]. Q1 2026 operating expenses of $4.36M (including $2.77M in advertising) against $1.81M revenue produce a $2.47M quarterly loss. The CEO received $800K in compensation in Q1 alone — 64% of quarterly revenue [2]. The company raised $5.3M via ATM offering in Q1 2026, increasing shares outstanding by 43% (7.12M to 10.22M), with 4.5M warrants at $3.14 creating further overhang. Cash of $1.51M plus $10.2M in corporate bonds provides roughly 12 months of runway at the current burn rate, but the bonds carry unrealized losses of $358K. The strategy is scattered across AI tools, health, defense, gaming, and spiritual apps with no focus. All AI products are thin wrappers over commoditizing third-party AI models with no proprietary technology moat [1][2].
key_catalysts: America First Defense.AI's Anduril Lattice Program integration and Naval Postgraduate School drone hijacker license could unlock defense sector revenue significantly exceeding the $2.9M investment [1]
AWS-funded AI Sales Coaching Platform with Caylent development partner could gain enterprise traction in the growing AI-enabled sales enablement market [1]
ByteDance partnership accelerating Art-Gen.AI monetization through preferred pricing and early access to next-generation video models, potentially driving subscription revenue growth [1]
key_risks: Continuous dilution is the primary funding mechanism — shares increased 43% in Q1 2026 alone via ATM offering, with 4.5M warrants at $3.14 creating further overhang, and the $2.47M quarterly burn rate ensures continued issuance [1]
CEO compensation of $800K in Q1 2026 ($400K salary + $400K bonus) against $1.81M revenue and 3 employees raises governance concerns about resource allocation priorities [2]

All AI products (UnGPT, Art-Gen, Bible Pray AI, Sales Coaching) are thin application wrappers over third-party AI models with no proprietary technology — as OpenAI, Google, and ByteDance expand vertically, the middleman position faces existential commoditization risk [1]
sources: Gaxos.ai Q1 2026 10-Q Filing, FY2025 10-K, Press Releases (ByteDance Partnership, AWS Funding, Defense.AI Anduril Lattice, Drone Hijacker License), 2025-2026
